jokes.text: Son of a bitch! Guys, guys, guys. Oh. Are you okay? Oh! I think you broke your nose.
jokes.setup: Character appears to have injured themselves off-screen
jokes.punchline: Frank's broken nose becomes a running visual gag throughout the episode
jokes.explanation: Classic physical comedy setup that establishes Frank's deteriorating appearance as a visual running gag

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Son of a bitch!

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Guys, guys, guys.
Oh.
Are you okay?
Oh!
I think you broke your nose.

jokes.text: Oh, strange man, titty bar.
jokes.setup: Frank is explaining how he got into this mess
jokes.punchline: His casual, abbreviated summary of meeting a shady character
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank delivery - the economy of language and matter-of-fact tone about something obviously sketchy is excellent character work

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Oh, strange man, titty bar.

jokes.text: Because you make a lot of cash, and you ogle some broads.
jokes.setup: Someone asks why Frank wanted to do a beauty pageant
jokes.punchline: Frank's characteristically crude and honest motivation
jokes.explanation: Perfectly in character for Frank to state his sleazy motivations so bluntly

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Because you make a lot of cash, and you ogle some broads.

jokes.text: Oh! Looks like somebody forgot their swimsuit.
jokes.setup: Gang expects adult women in swimsuits to walk through the door
jokes.punchline: Children enter instead, subverting expectations dramatically
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection that completely changes the premise and creates the central conflict of the episode

dialogue.line: Oh! Looks like somebody forgot their swimsuit.

jokes.text: That's the problem.
jokes.setup: Children have just entered for what they thought was an adult pageant
jokes.punchline: Frank's understated reaction to the massive problem this creates
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing and understatement for what is obviously a huge problem

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: That's the problem.

jokes.text: How would you win pageants? Did you have a different face?
jokes.setup: Dee claims she used to win beauty pageants as a child
jokes.punchline: Brutal question about her current appearance
jokes.explanation: Savage and economical burn with perfect setup-punchline structure. Very quotable.

dialogue.line: How would you win pageants? Did you have a different face?

jokes.text: No, she was a dog back then, too.
jokes.setup: Following up on the 'different face' joke
jokes.punchline: Mac doubles down by saying she was always ugly
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the insult, though somewhat predictable follow-up

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: No, she was a dog back then, too.

jokes.text: In other countries, okay, women that can't show their ankles, right? They got to be in those big, black tarps or whatever. In America, we can show toddlers in tiny bikinis
jokes.setup: Charlie starts what seems like a patriotic comparison
jokes.punchline: The comparison reveals something disturbing about American culture
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy that uses Charlie's twisted logic to expose something genuinely disturbing about pageant culture

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: In other countries, okay, women that can't show their ankles, right? They got to be in those big, black tarps or whatever. In America, we can show toddlers in tiny bikinis

jokes.text: We can do whatever we want with our kids. And that's what America is all about.
jokes.setup: Mac gets swept up in Charlie's twisted patriotic logic
jokes.punchline: His phrasing makes it sound even more sinister
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that makes the implications even darker through poor word choice

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: We can do whatever we want with our kids. And that's what America is all about.

jokes.text: U.S.A. U.S.A., U.S.A., U.S.A., U.S.A., U.S.A.!
jokes.setup: Gang has convinced themselves that child pageants are patriotic
jokes.punchline: They break into a USA chant over exploiting children
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurd escalation showing how the gang can convince themselves of anything

dialogue.line: U.S.A. U.S.A., U.S.A., U.S.A., U.S.A., U.S.A.!

jokes.text: I am not gonna diddle your kids. I'm not like that. That's not my thing. I met that guy in a titty bar.
jokes.setup: Frank tries to reassure the parents
jokes.punchline: His attempt at reassurance makes him sound more suspicious
jokes.explanation: Classic cringe comedy where trying to appear innocent makes you look guilty. Very quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I am not gonna diddle your kids. I'm not like that. That's not my thing. I met that guy in a titty bar.

jokes.text: Do I look suspicious? You look grotesque.
jokes.setup: Frank asks if he looks suspicious with his broken nose
jokes.punchline: Dee's brutal honesty about his appearance
jokes.explanation: Good callback to his injury with Dee's characteristically harsh response

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Do I look suspicious?

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: You look grotesque.

jokes.text: We got to definitely write a song about how we do not diddle kids.
jokes.setup: Charlie thinks they need to address the diddling accusations
jokes.punchline: His solution is characteristically backwards
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Charlie logic - his solution makes the problem worse. Highly quotable and original.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: We got to definitely write a song about how we do not diddle kids.

jokes.text: There is no quicker way for people to think that you are diddling kids than by writing a song about it.
jokes.setup: Charlie suggests writing a song about not diddling kids
jokes.punchline: Mac points out the obvious flaw in this plan
jokes.explanation: Excellent straight-man response that points out the absurdity. Very quotable wisdom.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: There is no quicker way for people to think that you are diddling kids than by writing a song about it.

jokes.text: But there's a guy. Uh, uh, there's a fat guy. Well, his face is busted to bits, and he... he's in charge. Real jacked up face and fat.
jokes.setup: Charlie tries to deflect the inspector to Frank
jokes.punchline: His description of Frank is brutally accurate but inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Charlie's characteristically blunt and inappropriate description of Frank

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: But there's a guy. Uh, uh, there's a fat guy. Well, his face is busted to bits, and he... he's in charge. Real jacked up face and fat.

jokes.text: Yeah, those mothers just left their kids here in a bar with complete strangers in a pageant that's under investigation. They're terrible parents.
jokes.setup: The gang discusses the child protection inspector
jokes.punchline: Dee points out the obvious parental negligence
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor that highlights the absurdity of the situation

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Yeah, those mothers just left their kids here in a bar with complete strangers in a pageant that's under investigation. They're terrible parents.

jokes.text: I see amateurs, I see trash, little pieces of trash on my stage. You guys are dummies.
jokes.setup: Dee is directing the children in the pageant
jokes.punchline: She verbally abuses children like a Hollywood director
jokes.explanation: Excellent cringe comedy showing Dee's complete inappropriateness with children

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: I see amateurs, I see trash, little pieces of trash on my stage. You guys are dummies.

jokes.text: Samantha gets to be mean because Samantha is a star, okay? Maybe one day you'll be a star, but right now you're not. You're junk.
jokes.setup: A child complains that another child is being mean
jokes.punchline: Dee justifies the mean behavior and continues the abuse
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Dee's inappropriate behavior, very in character

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Samantha gets to be mean because Samantha is a star, okay? Maybe one day you'll be a star, but right now you're not. You're junk.

jokes.text: You belong in the bathroom. Go!
jokes.setup: A child says they need to go to the bathroom
jokes.punchline: Dee uses it as another opportunity to insult them
jokes.explanation: Continues the pattern of Dee being awful to children, though somewhat repetitive

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: You belong in the bathroom. Go!

jokes.text: You're ugly. You don't-- that's not something you say. You're mean. You're the meanest girl in the world.
jokes.setup: Dee tries to compliment the mean child Samantha
jokes.punchline: Samantha brutally insults Dee back
jokes.explanation: Excellent reversal where the child out-cruels Dee, showing her getting a taste of her own medicine

dialogue.speaker: Samantha
dialogue.line: You're ugly.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: You don't-- that's not something you say. You're mean. You're the meanest girl in the world.

jokes.text: All hair is technically dead, you stupid idiot smarty-pants stupid, stupid idiot.
jokes.setup: Samantha insults Dee's hair
jokes.punchline: Dee's response devolves into childish name-calling despite starting with a fact
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Dee reduced to a child's level, starting smart then deteriorating

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: All hair is technically dead, you stupid idiot smarty-pants stupid, stupid idiot.

jokes.text: Terrible breath. Wheezing.
jokes.setup: Dee tries to tell a knock-knock joke
jokes.punchline: Samantha interrupts with accurate observations about Dee's physical state
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of the child destroying Dee with simple observations

dialogue.line: Terrible breath. Wheezing.

jokes.text: This is what America is about, okay, being able to eat at any rate you want to eat.
jokes.setup: Someone tells Charlie to slow down eating
jokes.punchline: Charlie turns basic eating into a constitutional right
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback to Charlie's tendency to make everything about American freedom

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: This is what America is about, okay, being able to eat at any rate you want to eat.

jokes.text: Your mom doesn't know dick. She's a dumb, fat cow, and your sister-- she is a stupid little shit-mouthed bitch, isn't she?
jokes.setup: Charlie tries to build up a young girl's confidence
jokes.punchline: His method involves vulgarly insulting the girl's family
jokes.explanation: Excellent cringe comedy showing Charlie's completely inappropriate attempt at motivation

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Your mom doesn't know dick. She's a dumb, fat cow, and your sister-- she is a stupid little shit-mouthed bitch, isn't she?

jokes.text: I know, but it's not what you care about, it's what I care about.
jokes.setup: The girl says she doesn't care about winning
jokes.punchline: Charlie reveals his selfish motivations
jokes.explanation: Perfect distillation of the gang's selfishness, even when supposedly helping children

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I know, but it's not what you care about, it's what I care about.

jokes.text: I had it done at a funeral home. Where in the hell am I going to go-- to the goddamn makeup counter at Macy's? You go to a funeral home to get gruesome repairs.
jokes.setup: Someone asks why Frank got his makeup done at a funeral home
jokes.punchline: Frank's logic for why funeral homes are the right choice for his injuries
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Frank logic - completely absurd but delivered with total confidence

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I had it done at a funeral home. Where in the hell am I going to go-- to the goddamn makeup counter at Macy's? You go to a funeral home to get gruesome repairs.

jokes.text: You look like you're at your own wake.
jokes.setup: Frank's funeral home makeup job
jokes.punchline: Dee's accurate assessment of how he looks
jokes.explanation: Good visual callback with Dee delivering the perfect description

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: You look like you're at your own wake.

jokes.text: Go into the toilet and run your mouth under the sink.
jokes.setup: Frank says his mouth is dry
jokes.punchline: His solution is characteristically gross and inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Typical Frank grossness, very in character

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Go into the toilet and run your mouth under the sink.

jokes.text: I figure he's a cretin. Why would I have a cretin like that near me if I have something to hide?
jokes.setup: Frank brought the mortician to the pageant
jokes.punchline: His backwards logic for why this makes him look innocent
jokes.explanation: Classic Frank logic - completely backwards but delivered with conviction

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I figure he's a cretin. Why would I have a cretin like that near me if I have something to hide?

jokes.text: Which one of these talented, uh, entertainers, who I am not attracted to at all, will be the winner? I'm not attracted to any of them, none of them.
jokes.setup: Frank tries to host the pageant professionally
jokes.punchline: His over-emphasis on not being attracted makes him sound more suspicious
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Frank's suspicious behavior, though somewhat repetitive

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Which one of these talented, uh, entertainers, who I am not attracted to at all, will be the winner? I'm not attracted to any of them, none of them.

jokes.text: A big, humungous pain in my Vagina...
jokes.setup: Justine performs a song about moms being annoying
jokes.punchline: The song escalates to inappropriate language from a child
jokes.explanation: Shock value comedy from inappropriate child performance, gets reaction but not particularly clever

dialogue.speaker: Justine
dialogue.line: A big, humungous pain in my Vagina...

jokes.text: Mr. Gorbachev, tear down this wall. The only thing we have to fear is... fear itself.
jokes.setup: Benjamin's patriotic song performance
jokes.punchline: Random presidential quotes mixed into the song
jokes.explanation: Mildly amusing absurdist moment but not particularly strong

dialogue.line: Mr. Gorbachev, tear down this wall. The only thing we have to fear is... fear itself.

jokes.text: You bang the dead bodies? I don't give a shit. If I was dead, you could bang me all you want. Who cares? Dead body's like a piece of trash.
jokes.setup: Frank asks the mortician about necrophilia
jokes.punchline: Frank's casual attitude about posthumous abuse
jokes.explanation: Very dark Frank moment, disturbing but in character

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: You bang the dead bodies? I don't give a shit. If I was dead, you could bang me all you want. Who cares? Dead body's like a piece of trash.

jokes.text: Fill me up with cream. Make a stew out of my ass. What's the big deal? Bang me, eat me, grind me up into little pieces, throw me in the river.
jokes.setup: Frank continues his death philosophy
jokes.punchline: Increasingly disturbing suggestions for what to do with his corpse
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Frank's disturbing death monologue

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Fill me up with cream. Make a stew out of my ass. What's the big deal? Bang me, eat me, grind me up into little pieces, throw me in the river.

jokes.text: That was a mistake. The janitor got a hold of the P.A. system. Puerto Rican guy.
jokes.setup: Frank's disturbing speech was broadcast over the PA
jokes.punchline: Frank tries to blame it on a fictional janitor with casual racism
jokes.explanation: Frank's attempt to cover his mistake with typical inappropriate deflection

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: That was a mistake. The janitor got a hold of the P.A. system. Puerto Rican guy.

jokes.text: Man's a diddler. Sick son of a bitch goes around all these kids pageants, pretends he's an inspector. We've been looking for him for a while.
jokes.setup: The gang worries they're in trouble with the law
jokes.punchline: The inspector they trusted was actually the predator
jokes.explanation: Excellent plot twist that turns the entire episode's paranoia on its head

dialogue.speaker: Police Officer
dialogue.line: Man's a diddler. Sick son of a bitch goes around all these kids pageants, pretends he's an inspector. We've been looking for him for a while.

jokes.text: So, there's another unrelated diddler in the mix?
jokes.setup: Learning Walter was actually a predator
jokes.punchline: Dennis's casual reaction to discovering another predator
jokes.explanation: Great deadpan reaction to absurd situation

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: So, there's another unrelated diddler in the mix?

jokes.text: So, there's another unrelated diddler in the mix?
jokes.setup: Learning that Walter was the real predator
jokes.punchline: Dennis's matter-of-fact question about the coincidence
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan response to the absurd coincidence

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: So, there's another unrelated diddler in the mix?

jokes.text: It's like throwing a picnic at the beach and getting pissed when the seagulls show up.
jokes.setup: Explaining why predators show up at children's events
jokes.punchline: Disturbing but accurate analogy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark analogy that's both funny and disturbingly true. Very quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Police Officer
dialogue.line: It's like throwing a picnic at the beach and getting pissed when the seagulls show up.

jokes.text: I tell you, children's beauty pageants are an American tradition, but not a proud one.
jokes.setup: Officer reflecting on children's pageants
jokes.punchline: Understatement about the problems with pageants
jokes.explanation: Good callback that undermines the earlier patriotic justification

dialogue.speaker: Police Officer
dialogue.line: I tell you, children's beauty pageants are an American tradition, but not a proud one.
